title: Track User First Login and Last Login Dates
description:
Add first login and last login date tracking to the user model to support a re-engagement drip campaign for users who haven't returned to the product.

### Query
```sql
SELECT id, email, first_login_at, last_login_at
FROM dtp_users
WHERE first_login_at IS NOT NULL OR last_login_at IS NOT NULL;
```

The existing `created_at` field represents the first authentication/authorization event. The new fields specifically track login activity.
acceptance_criteria: dtp_users table includes first_login_at and last_login_at columns
first_login_at is set on the user's first login and never updated
last_login_at is updated on every subsequent login
Login dates can be queried for the drip campaign
middleware_changes: Authentication flow updated to set first_login_at on initial login and update last_login_at on every login.
data_model_changes: Added first_login_at and last_login_at datetime columns to dtp_users table.
implementation_notes: Two new fields added to the user model: first_login_at and last_login_at. The existing created_at field tracks initial account creation during first authentication. New fields specifically track login events for the drip campaign use case.
comment_summary: Waqar added first_login_at and last_login_at fields to the user model and provided the SQL query. Noted that created_at already tracks initial authentication but the new fields specifically track login activity.
